outcome_contract.why: The owner dashboard was built as part of the prime app wave 1 (TASK-47, TASK-48, TASK-49) but the aggregation writer (TASK-47 scheduled job) was never implemented. The dashboard returns empty/zero data indefinitely without it.
outcome_contract.intended_outcome_statement: Owner dashboard shows non-zero, real guest KPI data after the aggregation writer runs for the first time. The `ownerKpis/{date}` nodes in Firebase RTDB are populated for recent dates.

engineering_coverage.applicable: N/A — dashboard UX unchanged; empty-state resolves when data is written

C2: role-gated writes via custom token; no rule weakening; service account UID registered with `admin` role (satisfies both `preArrival` read and `ownerKpis` write rules); endpoint protected by `PRIME_KPI_AGGREGATION_SECRET` bearer token
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Service account setup + endpoint secret required before deploy; `admin` role is narrowest role satisfying both rule sets

engineering_coverage.applicable: Writer logs: date, guestCount, success/failure, Firebase write errors; `console.error` on failure (matches existing function pattern)
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Same, but less isolated (aggregator redesign obscures I/O boundary)
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Structured logging in writer CF Function; no new alerting infrastructure

engineering_coverage.applicable: B1: aggregator tests untouched; new projection unit tests + writer endpoint tests added
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: B2: all aggregator tests must be rewritten
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: 2 new test files: projection shim unit tests, writer endpoint tests (Firebase mocked)

engineering_coverage.applicable: B1: `RawDayData` interface unchanged; projection shim is a new adapter layer; `DailyKpiRecord` write contract unchanged
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: B2: `RawDayData` interface redesigned; all consumers updated
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Projection shim is the only new contract surface; `kpiAggregator.ts` is frozen

engineering_coverage.applicable: A1 primary (daily): O(rooms × bookingRefs) for `roomsByDate` traversal + O(guestUuids × (6 + N_requests)) per-UUID reads (including `occupantIndex`); A2 fallback (backfill/archived): O(all bookings) scan filtered in-memory, then same per-UUID reads; both paths idempotent upsert
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: A2 primary every run — unbounded growth; unacceptable
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Daily run: ~90 reads for 10 guests × 2 requests avg — well within Firebase free tier; fallback scan acceptable for one-time backfill only

engineering_coverage.applicable: Additive CF Pages Function; no changes to existing routes; GitHub Actions cron can be disabled in seconds; RTDB writes are idempotent (re-run safe); rollback = disable cron + delete `ownerKpis/` subtree in Firebase console
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: D2 (separate Worker) requires a wrangler delete for rollback
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Zero-risk rollout: new endpoint, new cron; existing guest flows untouched

open_operator_questions: Will you create the `messagingUsers/{svcUid}` entry in RTDB with `role: 'admin'` and confirm that `PRIME_FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_EMAIL` + `PRIME_FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRIVATE_KEY` are set in CF Pages?
Will you add `PRIME_KPI_AGGREGATION_SECRET` as a CF Pages secret and as a GitHub Actions repository secret?

stage_payload.decision_summary: The owner dashboard (`/owner`, `/owner/scorecard`) is fully built and wired to a read layer that reads `ownerKpis/{date}` from Firebase RTDB. Nothing ever writes to those nodes. Three architectural decisions must be resolved before the writer can be built: (1) how to enumerate guests for a given date without a `checkInDate` index, (2) how to bridge the gap between the `kpiAggregator.ts` `RawDayData` interface and the actual RTDB multi-root schema, and (3) how to authenticate server-side writes against the role-gated `ownerKpis` rule.
goals: Choose a guest enumeration strategy that avoids a full `bookings` scan.
Choose a data projection approach (aggregator redesign vs projection shim) that preserves existing tests.
Choose an auth approach for server-side RTDB writes.
Choose a trigger mechanism (cron vs on-demand endpoint) that ships with minimal infrastructure delta.
constraints: Cloudflare Pages does not support cron triggers natively.
`FirebaseRest` (REST-based, Workers-compatible) is the required write pattern — no Firebase Admin SDK.
`ownerKpis` RTDB write rule requires `messagingUsers/{uid}/role === 'owner' OR 'admin' OR 'developer'`.
`bookings` node does not index `checkInDate` — full scan without an alternative index.

evaluation_criteria.Why it matters: `kpiAggregator.ts` tests are the primary correctness guarantee for KPI computation — must not break

evaluation_criteria.Criterion: Minimal Firebase read cost per run
evaluation_criteria.Why it matters: Hostel scale is small but disciplined cost control is a project value
`ownerKpis` data is business-sensitive; write access must be role-gated
Shipping speed matters — simpler is better when outcomes are equivalent
Must be runnable manually (backfill) and daily (ongoing)
First run writes real data to RTDB — should be recoverable if wrong

options_considered.Upside: O(rooms × bookingRefs) read per date; no full scan; reliable for recent dates
options_considered.Downside: Mutable: entries removed on archive/delete — incomplete for historical backfill of archived dates
options_considered.Key risks: Silent miss for backfill without fallback; requires fallback path

options_considered.Description: Read entire `bookings` node, filter in-memory by `checkInDate`
options_considered.Upside: Complete record regardless of archival state; reliable fallback for backfill
options_considered.Downside: O(all bookings ever) — grows unbounded; expensive for daily use
options_considered.Key risks: Acceptable as backfill-only fallback, not daily path
Minor rule deploy; would enable O(1) date-filtered query

options_considered.Description: Build a new `projectGuestKpiData(date, guestUuids, firebase)` function in the writer layer that reads the 6 RTDB roots per UUID and returns `RawDayData`. `aggregateDailyKpis()` unchanged.
options_considered.Upside: All existing aggregator tests pass without modification. Clean separation: projection (I/O) vs aggregation (pure).
options_considered.Downside: Additional function to write and test.
options_considered.Key risks: Projection correctness must be verified independently.

options_considered.Option: B2: Redesign `aggregateDailyKpis()` to accept real RTDB types
options_considered.Description: Change the function signature to take multi-root data structures directly. Update all tests.
options_considered.Upside: Single function boundary; no projection layer indirection.
options_considered.Downside: All 5+ `kpiAggregator.ts` tests must be rewritten. Breaks the pure/IO separation. Increases blast radius.
options_considered.Key risks: Existing tests are the ground truth for KPI computation accuracy — losing them risks silent regressions in KPI values.

options_considered.Option: C1: Relax RTDB rule for `ownerKpis` writes (server-to-server)
options_considered.Description: Change the `ownerKpis` write rule to allow unauthenticated writes OR allow writes with just `CF_FIREBASE_API_KEY`
options_considered.Upside: Simplest implementation — no new credential management
options_considered.Downside: Weakens RTDB security model; `ownerKpis` data is business-sensitive; any authenticated user could overwrite it
options_considered.Key risks: High — opens write access to anyone who can obtain the database URL

options_considered.Option: C2: Pre-register service UID in `messagingUsers` with `admin` role + use custom token flow
options_considered.Description: Create a stable service account UID (e.g., `svc-kpi-aggregator`); register it in `messagingUsers/{svcUid}` with `role: 'admin'`; use `PRIME_FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_EMAIL` + `PRIME_FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRIVATE_KEY` (existing secret contract) via `firebase-custom-token.ts` to generate a custom JWT; exchange via Identity Toolkit for an ID token; use the ID token as the RTDB `auth=` credential
options_considered.Upside: Follows existing auth pattern (`staff-auth-session.ts`). `admin` role satisfies both `preArrival` read and `ownerKpis` write. No rule changes needed. Uses existing secret names.
options_considered.Downside: Requires operator to: (a) confirm `PRIME_FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_EMAIL` + `PRIME_FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRIVATE_KEY` are set in CF Pages; (b) create `messagingUsers/{svcUid}` entry with `role: 'admin'` in RTDB once. Writer must implement custom-token → ID-token exchange step.
options_considered.Key risks: One-time setup; token exchange adds one extra API call (Identity Toolkit) per run

options_considered.Option: C3: Relax rule only for `ownerKpis` writes from a fixed IP / CF IP range
options_considered.Description: Not feasible — CF Pages doesn't have a stable IP range
A workflow sends `POST /api/aggregate-kpis` with bearer auth daily at e.g. 02:00 UTC
options_considered.Upside: No new CF infrastructure. Works immediately. Free. Backfill by running workflow manually with date param.
options_considered.Downside: Depends on GitHub Actions availability for production daily operations.
options_considered.Key risks: GitHub Actions has rare outages; missing one day is tolerable (idempotent write can backfill).

options_considered.Option: D2: Separate Cloudflare Worker with cron trigger
options_considered.Description: A new Worker in the monorepo with `[triggers] crons = ["0 2 * * *"]` calls the aggregation logic
options_considered.Upside: True server-side cron; no GitHub dependency
options_considered.Downside: New Worker resource to deploy, manage, and pay for. Duplicates prime's CF infrastructure.
options_considered.Key risks: Added operational surface area for a small benefit.

options_considered.Option: D3: On-demand CF Pages Function only (no automatic trigger)
options_considered.Description: Endpoint callable manually; no cron wired
options_considered.Upside: Immediate unblock for backfill and manual runs. Ships fastest.
options_considered.Downside: Dashboard only shows data if someone remembers to run it.
options_considered.Key risks: Operational debt — must be remembered to run daily.
options_considered.Viable?: Partial (start here, layer D1 on top)

chosen_approach.Read cost: ≤ 50 guests × (1 occupantIndex + 1 booking + 1 preArrival + 1 checkins + N_requests_byGuest + N_requests_byId + 1 bagStorage) reads per run. For 10 guests × 2 requests avg: ~90 reads/day. Acceptable for a daily job.
chosen_approach.Operator creates `messagingUsers/{svcUid: 'admin'` in RTDB (one-time setup; required before production auth works).
chosen_approach.**A2 (full `bookings` scan) as primary**: unbounded cost, violates read-budget discipline. Retained as automatic fallback for backfill of archived dates when `roomsByDate` is empty.
chosen_approach.**B2 (redesign aggregator)** — Rejected: breaks existing tests, increases blast radius, removes the pure/IO separation.
chosen_approach.**C2 with `developer` role** — Rejected: `developer` role cannot read `preArrival/{uuid}` (only `staff|admin|owner` can). Service account needs `admin` role to satisfy both the `preArrival` read rule and `ownerKpis` write rule simultaneously.
chosen_approach.**D2 (separate CF Worker)** — Rejected: unnecessary infrastructure overhead for a daily batch job; GitHub Actions cron achieves the same with less.
chosen_approach.Q: Will you add `PRIME_KPI_AGGREGATION_SECRET` as a CF Pages secret and as a GitHub Actions repository secret?
chosen_approach.Why operator input is required: This is a shared bearer secret that the operator must generate and register in two places.
chosen_approach.Planning impact: Without this, TASK-C's endpoint cannot be called securely from TASK-D's cron workflow.

rejected_approaches: **A2 (full `bookings` scan) as primary** — Rejected as the primary daily path: unbounded cost, violates read-budget discipline. Retained as automatic fallback for backfill of archived dates when `roomsByDate` is empty.
**B2 (redesign aggregator)** — Rejected: breaks existing tests, increases blast radius, removes the pure/IO separation.
**C1 (relax RTDB rule)** — Rejected: unacceptable security regression; `ownerKpis` is business-sensitive.

**C2 with `developer` role** — Rejected: `developer` role cannot read `preArrival/{uuid}` (only `staff|admin|owner` can). Service account needs `admin` role to satisfy both the `preArrival` read rule and `ownerKpis` write rule simultaneously.
**D2 (separate CF Worker)** — Rejected: unnecessary infrastructure overhead for a daily batch job; GitHub Actions cron achieves the same with less.

validation_implications: Writer unit tests use the same Firebase mock pattern as existing `aggregate-read-path.test.tsx`.
Projection shim tests verify correct RTDB path construction and data mapping for each of the 6 signal roots.
sequencing_constraints: TASK-A (RTDB setup + secrets) must complete before TASK-C can be tested in production.
TASK-B (projection shim) must precede TASK-C (writer endpoint).
TASK-C must deploy before TASK-D (cron) makes any sense.
TASK-E (access gate) can be done any time after TASK-C deploys.
risks_to_carry_forward: `roomsByDate/{date}` may be empty for dates before the reception app started populating it — backfill (TASK-F) must handle gracefully (log warning, write zero-record, do not fail).

Custom token exchange involves two API calls (custom JWT generation + Identity Toolkit `signInWithCustomToken` exchange) — must be factored into CF Function timeout budget. Token should be cached for the duration of a single run.

`primeRequests/byGuest/{uuid}` stores boolean values keyed by request ID — the full request object (including `type`) lives at `primeRequests/byId/{requestId}`. Projection shim must traverse both paths to classify `extension` vs `bag_drop` requests for the count fields.
The endpoint bearer secret (`PRIME_KPI_AGGREGATION_SECRET`) must exist in both CF Pages and GitHub Actions secrets before TASK-C deploys and TASK-D wires up.

areas.label: KPI write path
areas.current: Does not exist; `ownerKpis/` subtree is empty
areas.trigger: GitHub Actions cron fires at 02:00 UTC (or manual POST call)
areas.future: 1. GitHub Actions sends `POST /api/aggregate-kpis` with body `{"date":"YYYY-MM-DD"}` and `Authorization: Bearer <PRIME_KPI_AGGREGATION_SECRET>` header (previous day by default). 2. CF Pages Function validates bearer secret, then authenticates to Firebase: calls `firebase-custom-token.ts` with `PRIME_FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_EMAIL` + `PRIME_FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRIVATE_KEY` → custom JWT → exchanges via Identity Toolkit for Firebase ID token → uses ID token as RTDB `auth=` credential. 3. Reads `roomsByDate/{date}/{room}/{bookingRef}/guestIds[]` → collects all guest UUIDs; if empty falls back
areas.steady: `kpiReader.ts` read path unchanged; dashboard pages unchanged; aggregation pure functions unchanged
areas.seams: Service account secrets (`PRIME_FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_*`) must be in CF Pages; `messagingUsers/{svcUid}` with `role: 'admin'` must exist; `PRIME_KPI_AGGREGATION_SECRET` must be in CF Pages and GitHub Actions

areas.label: KPI read path
areas.future: No change — read path already works; dashboard now shows real data instead of zeros

areas.label: Owner dashboard access gate
areas.future: Operator sets `NEXT_PUBLIC_ENABLE_STAFF_OWNER_ROUTES=true` in CF Pages dashboard; owner pages become accessible in production
areas.seams: Must be done separately; otherwise dashboard shows `StaffOwnerDisabledNotice` despite populated data
